back_translation: Darius the Mede received the kingdom when he was about sixty-two years old. (MT 6:1 = English/BSB 5:31)
It pleased Darius to appoint 120 satraps to rule throughout the kingdom,
and over them three administrators, of whom Daniel was one, so that the satraps would give account to them and the king would suffer no loss.
This Daniel distinguished himself above the administrators and satraps, because he had an excellent spirit; so the king planned to set him over the whole kingdom.

So the administrators and satraps tried to find a charge against Daniel concerning the affairs of the kingdom, but they could find no charge or corruption, because he was trustworthy, and no negligence or corruption was found in him.
Then these men said, 'We will never find any charge against this Daniel unless we find something against him in the law of his God.'
So the administrators and satraps came thronging to the king and said, 'O King Darius, may you live forever!

All the royal administrators, prefects, satraps, advisers, and governors have agreed that the king should issue a decree and establish an ordinance that anyone who petitions any god or man within thirty days, except you, O king, shall be thrown into the den of lions.
Now, O king, establish the decree and sign the document so that it cannot be changed, according to the law of the Medes and Persians, which cannot be repealed.'
So King Darius signed the document and the decree.

When Daniel learned that the document had been signed, he went into his house, where the windows of his upper room opened toward Jerusalem, and three times a day he knelt down, prayed, and gave thanks before his God, just as he had done before.
Then these men came together and found Daniel petitioning and imploring before his God.

So they approached the king and asked about the royal decree: 'Did you not sign a decree that anyone who petitions any god or man within thirty days, except you, O king, shall be thrown into the den of lions?' The king answered, 'This stands according to the law of the Medes and Persians, which cannot be repealed.'
Then they said to the king, 'Daniel, one of the exiles from Judah, pays no regard to you, O king, or to the decree you signed; he still prays three times a day.'
When the king heard this, he was greatly distressed and resolved to deliver Daniel; he labored to find a way to rescue him until sundown.
Then those men came together to the king and said, 'Know, O king, that by the law of the Medes and Persians no decree or ordinance the king establishes can be changed.'
So the king gave the command, and they brought Daniel and threw him into the den of lions. The king said to Daniel, 'May your God, whom you serve continually, deliver you!'
A stone was brought and laid over the mouth of the den, and the king sealed it with his own signet ring and with the rings of his nobles, so that nothing concerning Daniel could be changed.
Then the king went back to his palace, fasted through the night, allowed no entertainment to be brought before him, and could not sleep.
At the first light of dawn the king rose early and hurried to the den of lions in agitation.
As he drew near the den, he cried out to Daniel in a voice of grief, 'O Daniel, servant of the living God, has your God, whom you serve continually, been able to deliver you from the lions?'
Daniel said to the king, 'O king, may you live forever!
My God sent His angel to shut the mouths of the lions, so they did not harm me, because He found me innocent; and against you also, O king, I have done no wrong.'
The king was overjoyed and commanded that Daniel be lifted out of the den. When Daniel was lifted out, no wound whatsoever was found on him, because he had trusted in his God.

Then the king gave the command, and they brought the men who had falsely accused Daniel and threw them into the den of lions—them, their children, and their wives. And before they reached the bottom of the den, the lions overpowered them and crushed all their bones.
Then King Darius wrote to the people of every nation and language who dwell in all the earth: 'May your prosperity abound!

I issue a decree that in every part of my kingdom men are to tremble and fear before the God of Daniel, for He is the living God, enduring forever; His kingdom will never be destroyed, and His dominion will last to the end.
He delivers and rescues; He works signs and wonders in the heavens and on the earth. He is the one who delivered Daniel from the power of the lions.'
So this Daniel prospered during the reign of Darius and the reign of Cyrus the Persian. (MT 6:29 = English/BSB 6:28)
